heading: White Sands National Park
label: 410dddd White Sands National Park

Historical Marker Commemorating Effects of Radiation Exposure at Holloman Air Force Base and White Sands Missile Range, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–60, div. B, title XXVIII, § 2861139 Stat. 1324Dec. 18, 2025“(a)Historical Markers Required.—“(1).—Holloman air force baseThe Secretary of the Air Force shall place a historical marker as described in subsection (b) in a publicly accessible location at the Holloman Air Force Base.“(2).—White sands missile rangeThe Secretary of the Army shall place a historical marker as described in subsection (b) in a publicly accessible location at the White Sands Missile Range.“(b).—Required InformationA historical marker described in subsection (a) shall commemorate the effects of radiation exposure on communities in New Mexico as a result of the Manhattan Project and the nuclear test conducted at the Trinity Site. Such historical marker shall include, at a minimum, the following:“(1)An unclassified description of the history of the Manhattan Project and its purpose, including a description of the nuclear test conducted at the Trinity Site and an acknowledgement that it was the location of the world’s first nuclear weapons test.“(2)A description of how the classified nature of the Manhattan Project and the nuclear test conducted at the Trinity Site led to the unknowing exposure of individuals in communities located downwind from such testing to radiological byproducts and associated consequences of such byproducts.“(3)The markers will honor the resilience of the New Mexico communities during and after World War II, recognizing the service and sacrifice of all who contributed to the war effort.“(c).—Briefing RequiredNot later than 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of the Air Force and Secretary of the Army shall jointly provide to the Committees on Armed Services of the House of Representatives and Senate a briefing on the implementation of the requirements of this section.Dec. 18, 2025“(d).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—Manhattan projectThe term ‘Manhattan Project’ means the Federal military program to develop an atomic bomb ending on .December 31, 1946“(2).—Trinity siteThe term ‘Trinity Site’ means the location in the Jornada del Muerto desert near Alamogordo, New Mexico, on the former Alamorgordo Bombing and Gunnery Range, known today as Holloman Air Force Base and the White Sands Missile Range, where the world’s first nuclear weapon was exploded on .”July 16, 1945

children.heading: Map
children.label: 1 Map
children.text: The term “Map” means the map entitled “White Sands National Park Proposed Boundary Revision & Transfer of Lands Between National Park Service & Department of the Army”, numbered 142/136,271, and dated .February 14, 2017

children.heading: Military munitions
children.label: 2 Military munitions
children.text: The term “military munitions” has the meaning given the term in section 101(e) of title 10.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Missile range
children.label: 3 Missile range
children.text: The term “missile range” means the White Sands Missile Range, New Mexico, administered by the Secretary of the Army.

children.heading: Monument
children.label: 4 Monument
children.text: The term “Monument” means the White Sands National Monument, New Mexico, established by Presidential Proclamation No. 2025 ( note), dated , and administered by the Secretary of the Interior.54 U.S.C. 320301January 18, 1933

children.heading: Munitions debris
children.label: 5 Munitions debris
children.text: The term “munitions debris” has the meaning given the term in volume 8 of the Department of Defense Manual Number 6055.09-M entitled “DoD Ammunitions and Explosives Safety Standards” and dated (as in effect on ).February 29, 2008December 20, 2019

children.heading: Park
children.label: 6 Park
children.text: The term “Park” means the White Sands National Park established by subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Public land order
children.label: 7 Public land order
children.text: The term “Public Land Order” means Public Land Order 833, dated (17 Fed. Reg. 4822).May 21, 1952

children.heading: State
children.label: 8 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of New Mexico.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: To protect, preserve, and restore its scenic, scientific, educational, natural, geological, historical, cultural, archaeological, paleontological, hydrological, fish, wildlife, and recreational values and to enhance visitor experiences, there is established in the State the White Sands National Park as a unit of the National Park System.

children.heading: Abolishment of White Sands National Monument
children.label: 2 Abolishment of White Sands National Monument

children.heading: Abolishment
children.label: A Abolishment
children.text: Due to the establishment of the Park, the Monument is abolished.

children.heading: Incorporation
children.label: B Incorporation
children.text: The land and interests in land that comprise the Monument are incorporated in, and shall be considered to be part of, the Park.

children.heading: References
children.label: 3 References
children.text: Any reference in a law, map, regulation, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the “White Sands National Monument” shall be considered to be a reference to the “White Sands National Park”.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: 4 Availability of funds
children.text: Any funds available for the Monument shall be available for the Park.

children.label: A
children.text: this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: the laws generally applicable to units of the National Park System, including section 100101(a), chapter 1003, sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101, and chapter 3201 of title 54.

children.heading: County concurrence
children.label: A County concurrence
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall not submit a nomination for the Park to be included on the World Heritage List of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization unless each county in which the Park is located concurs in the nomination.

children.heading: Army notification
children.label: B Army notification
children.text: Before submitting a nomination for the Park to be included on the World Heritage List of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization, the Secretary of the Interior shall notify the Secretary of the Army of the intent of the Secretary of the Interior to nominate the Park.

children.label: A
children.text: valid existing rights (including water rights);

children.label: B
children.text: permits or contracts issued by the Monument;

children.label: C
children.text: existing agreements, including agreements with the Department of Defense;

children.label: D
children.text: the jurisdiction of the Department of Defense regarding the restricted airspace above the Park; or

children.label: E
children.text: the airshed classification of the Park under the Clean Air Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 7401

children.heading: Modification of Boundaries of White Sands National Park and White Sands Missile Range
children.label: c Modification of Boundaries of White Sands National Park and White Sands Missile Range

children.heading: Transfer of administrative jurisdiction to the Secretary of the Interior
children.label: A Transfer of administrative jurisdiction to the Secretary of the Interior

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Administrative jurisdiction over the land described in clause (ii) is transferred from the Secretary of the Army to the Secretary of the Interior.

children.label: I
children.text: the approximately 2,826 acres of land identified as “To NPS, lands inside current boundary” on the Map; and

children.label: II
children.text: the approximately 5,766 acres of land identified as “To NPS, new additions” on the Map.

children.heading: Transfer of administrative jurisdiction to the Secretary of the Army
children.label: B Transfer of administrative jurisdiction to the Secretary of the Army

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Administrative jurisdiction over the land described in clause (ii) is transferred from the Secretary of the Interior to the Secretary of the Army.

children.heading: Description of land
children.label: ii Description of land
children.text: The land referred to in clause (i) is the approximately 3,737 acres of land identified as “To DOA” on the Map.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The boundary of the Park is revised to reflect the boundary depicted on the Map.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior, in coordination with the Secretary of the Army, shall prepare and keep on file for public inspection in the appropriate office of the Secretary of the Interior a map and a legal description of the revised boundary of the Park.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: II Effect
children.text: The map and legal description under subclause (I) shall have the same force and effect as if included in this section, except that the Secretary of the Interior may correct clerical and typographical errors in the map and legal description.

children.heading: Boundary survey
children.label: iii Boundary survey
children.text: As soon as practicable after the date of the establishment of the Park and subject to the availability of funds, the Secretary of the Interior shall complete an official boundary survey of the Park.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The boundary of the missile range and the Public Land Order are modified to exclude the land transferred to the Secretary of the Interior under paragraph (1)(A) and to include the land transferred to the Secretary of the Army under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Map
children.label: ii Map
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall prepare a map and legal description depicting the revised boundary of the missile range.

children.heading: Park
children.label: A Park
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall administer the land transferred under paragraph (1)(A) in accordance with laws (including regulations) applicable to the Park.

children.heading: Missile range
children.label: B Missile range
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (C), the Secretary of the Army shall administer the land transferred to the Secretary of the Army under paragraph (1)(B) as part of the missile range.

children.heading: Infrastructure management
children.label: I Infrastructure management
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, in planning, constructing, and managing infrastructure on the land described in subclause (III), the Secretary of the Army shall apply low-impact development techniques and strategies to prevent impacts within the missile range and the Park from stormwater runoff from the land described in that subclause.

children.label: aa
children.text: manage the land described in subclause (III) in a manner consistent with the protection of natural and cultural resources within the missile range and the Park and in accordance with , division A of subtitle III of title 54, and the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act ( et seq.); andsection 670a(a)(1)(B) of this title25 U.S.C. 3001

children.label: bb
children.text: include the land described in subclause (III) in the integrated natural and cultural resource management plan for the missile range.

children.label: bb
children.text: T. 18 S., R. 5 E.; and

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Army shall continue to allow the Secretary of the Interior to maintain the fence shown on the Map until such time as the Secretary of the Interior determines that the fence is unnecessary for the management of the Park.

children.heading: Removal
children.label: II Removal
children.text: If the Secretary of the Interior determines that the fence is unnecessary for the management of the Park under subclause (I), the Secretary of the Interior shall promptly remove the fence at the expense of the Department of the Interior.

children.heading: Research
children.label: D Research
children.text: The Secretary of the Army and the Secretary of the Interior may enter into an agreement to allow the Secretary of the Interior to conduct certain research in the area identified as “Cooperative Use Research Area” on the Map.

children.heading: Military munitions and munitions debris
children.label: E Military munitions and munitions debris

children.heading: Response action
children.label: i Response action
children.text: With respect to any Federal liability, the Secretary of the Army shall remain responsible for any response action addressing military munitions or munitions debris on the land transferred under paragraph (1)(A) to the same extent as on the day before .December 20, 2019

children.heading: Investigation of military munitions and munitions debris
children.label: ii Investigation of military munitions and munitions debris

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior may request that the Secretary of the Army conduct 1 or more investigations of military munitions or munitions debris on any land transferred under paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: Access
children.label: II Access
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall give access to the Secretary of the Army to the land covered by a request under subclause (I) for the purposes of conducting the 1 or more investigations under that subclause.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: III Limitation
children.text: An investigation conducted under this clause shall be subject to available appropriations.

children.label: I
children.text: the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act of 1980 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 9601

children.label: II
children.text: the purposes for which the Park was established; and

children.label: III
children.text: any other applicable law.
